Label: Section 3.1
Issue: does not specify the types of web-ready devices supported
Correction: The DigitalHome system shall support web-ready devices including but not limited to computers, smartphones, and tablets.

Label: Section 3.2.1
Issue: does not specify the process for designating a Master User
Correction: The system shall provide an interface for the initial setup where the first user can designate a Master User.

Label: Section 3.4
Issue: does not specify the operating systems supported for the DH Home Web Server
Correction: The DH Home Web Server shall support operating systems including Windows, macOS, and Linux.

Label: Section 4.1
Issue: does not specify the types of small appliances that can be controlled
Correction: The system shall support control of small appliances such as coffee makers, lamps, and fans.

Label: Section 4.2
Issue: does not specify what happens if the temperature set point is outside the allowed range
Correction: If the temperature set point is outside the allowed range, the system shall notify the user and prevent the setting from being applied.

Label: Section 4.3
Issue: does not specify what happens if the humidity set point is outside the allowed range
Correction: If the humidity set point is outside the allowed range, the system shall notify the user and prevent the setting from being applied.

Label: Section 4.4
Issue: does not specify the types of alarms supported
Correction: The system shall support various types of alarms including audible alarms, visual alarms, and notifications via the web interface.

Label: Section 5.1
Issue: does not specify total supported number of concurrent users
Correction: The system shall handle up to 100 concurrent users without performance degradation.

Label: Section 5.2
Issue: does not specify the recovery time after a failure
Correction: The system shall recover and restore data within 5 minutes after a failure.

Label: Section 5.3
Issue: does not specify safety measures for power switches
Correction: The system shall include safety measures to prevent accidental activation of power switches, such as confirmation prompts for critical appliances.

Label: Section 5.4
Issue: does not specify the encryption standards used
Correction: The system shall use AES-256 encryption for data storage and TLS 1.2 for data transmission.

Label: Section 5.5
Issue: does not specify the frequency of maintenance updates
Correction: The system shall receive maintenance updates at least once every quarter.

Label: Section 5.7
Issue: does not specify the format of the user documentation
Correction: The user documentation shall be provided in HTML and PDF formats.

Label: N/A
Correction: The system shall be designed to scale horizontally to support additional devices and users as needed.

Label: N/A
Issue: missing section on user interface design guidelines
Correction: The user interface shall follow the HomeOwner Inc. design guidelines to ensure consistency and usability.
